name: Familial Hypercholesterolemia - Statin Therapy Query
description: Tests ability to find FH patients via statin (HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor) prescriptions. The PheKB FH algorithm uses a 30% LDL reduction adjustment for patients on lipid-lowering therapy. High-intensity statins (atorvastatin 40-80mg, rosuvastatin 20-40mg) are first-line for FH. Synthea distributes patients across 5 statins. An LLM querying only atorvastatin will miss patients on simvastatin/rosuvastatin/pravastatin/lovastatin.
prompt: Find patients prescribed statin medications used for cholesterol management, including atorvastatin, simvastatin, rosuvastatin, pravastatin, or lovastatin.

prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed any HMG-CoA reductase inhibitor (statin) for cholesterol management — atorvastatin, simvastatin, rosuvastatin, pravastatin, or lovastatin.
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any statin. Use these RxNorm ingredient codes: 83367 (atorvastatin), 36567 (simvastatin), 301542 (rosuvastatin), 42463 (pravastatin), 6472 (lovastatin). Combine in a single comma-separated code search.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with statin orders (Paths A and C). Statins have heavy cross-indication use beyond FH — they're first-line for general high cholesterol, secondary prevention after MI, and primary prevention based on ASCVD risk. Path C (statins for non-FH) is therefore very large.
